title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a keyed order and failover state machine in Go with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a keyed order and failover state machine in Go and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Ordering Scope Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 3466 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Ordering Scope Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-ordering-throughput-tradeoff-kezgly" that uses case 4989 to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Ordering Throughput Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go messaging team handles case 3564 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Ordering Throughput Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, keep Ordering Throughput Tradeoff connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
